name: Easy-on-Your-Heart Jambalaya
recipeIngredient: 1 Green bell pepper, med., stemmed, seeded and chopped
1/4 pound Turkey ham, cut into 1/2-inch pieces
10 ounces Oyster, fresh, shucked, small Pacific
1 pound Shrimp, medium (44-50 ct.), shelled and deveined
recipeInstructions:
1. In a 5 to 6 quart pan over high heat, combine onion, bell pepper, celery, garlic, and 2 cups water. Stir often until moisture evaporates and vegetables begin to stick to the pan, about 10 minutes.
2. Stir in an additional 2 tablespoons water. Add turkey sausage, bacon, and ham, and stir often until the mixture begins to brown, 8 to 10 minutes.
3. Stir in the bay leaf, cayenne, thyme, basil, pepper, parsley, rice, tomatoes, their liquid, broth, and juices drained from oysters. Bring to a boil over high heat, cover, and simmer until rice is tender to bite, 30 to 40 minutes.
4. Stir occasionally until shrimp turn pink, about 8 minutes; stir often.
5. Season to taste with salt. Makes 8 servings, each containing approximately 240 calories (20% from fat), 21 g protein, 5.4 g fat (1.5 g saturated), 28 g carbohydrates, 502 mg sodium, and 111 mg cholesterol.
